title: artemisia spicata subsp. eriantha
intro: Artemisia spicata subsp. eriantha, also known as sweet wormwood, is a subspecies of the wormwood plant native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimalarial properties. The health benefits of sweet wormwood include its ability to aid in the treatment of fever, digestive issues, and certain types of malaria. Sweet wormwood can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its essential oil is also used in herbal preparations to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
